description: Add a `chronicle doctor` command that performs a structured pre-flight health check across Config, Git, Agents, and Scheduler subsystems, reporting results with plain-English remediation hints, color symbols, and a stable --porcelain output mode.

userStories.title: CheckResult / CheckState types and all four check functions
userStories.description: Define the data model and pure check logic used by `chronicle doctor`. Create `CheckState` enum (Pass, Warn, Error, Skipped) and `CheckResult` struct (key, state, detail, hint). Implement the four check groups as standalone functions: `check_config` (file exists, valid TOML, git.remote non-empty), `check_git` (repo initialised, remote reachable with 5-second timeout, SSH key found — SSH remotes only), `check_agents` (sessions dir exists + file count, one entry per enabled agent), `check_scheduler` (crontab entry installed, lock absent or held by live non-stale PID). All check functions accept injected paths/state so they are unit-testable without a real filesystem or network. Write unit tests for every check covering pass, warn, error, and skipped states.
acceptanceCriteria: CheckState enum has Pass, Warn, Error, Skipped variants
CheckResult struct has key (&str or String), state (CheckState), detail (String), hint (Option<String>)
check_config returns 3 CheckResults: config.file, config.toml, config.remote
check_git returns up to 3 CheckResults: git.repo, git.remote (skipped when remote empty), git.ssh_key (skipped for HTTPS remotes)
check_agents returns one CheckResult per enabled agent (agents.pi, agents.claude) with file count in detail on pass
check_scheduler returns 2 CheckResults: scheduler.cron, scheduler.lock
Remote reachability check uses a 5-second connect timeout and reports error on timeout rather than hanging
SSH key check probes ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.ssh/id_ecdsa, ~/.ssh/id_rsa in order and passes if any is readable
All check functions have unit tests covering pass, warn/error, and skipped branches
cargo test passes; cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean; cargo fmt --check clean; cargo deny check clean
userStories.notes: Keep check functions in src/cli/mod.rs initially; extract to src/doctor/mod.rs only if the module grows beyond ~300 lines of check logic. Use existing StatusFormatter<W> color/TTY helpers from spec 002 rather than duplicating them.

userStories.title: DoctorArgs CLI wiring and human-readable output formatter
userStories.description: Wire `chronicle doctor` into the CLI and implement the human-readable output. Add `DoctorArgs { porcelain: bool, no_color: bool }` and a `Doctor` subcommand to the clap command tree in src/main.rs and src/cli/mod.rs. Implement `doctor_impl` (testable via generic writer) that runs all four check groups and emits grouped output: section headers (Config, Git, Agents, Scheduler), per-check lines with ✓/⚠/✗ symbols and optional remediation hints indented on the next line, and a summary line (`N errors · N warnings · N checks passed`). Apply the same color/TTY/NO_COLOR logic as `chronicle status`. Enforce exit codes: 0 all-pass, 1 warnings-only, 2 any-error.
acceptanceCriteria: chronicle doctor subcommand is registered and dispatches to doctor_impl
DoctorArgs has --porcelain and --no-color flags (--no-color and NO_COLOR env var suppress ANSI; --porcelain implies no color)
Output is grouped into four sections: Config, Git, Agents, Scheduler
Each check line is prefixed with ✓ (green), ⚠ (yellow), or ✗ (red) followed by the check description and detail
Each failing or warned check is followed by an indented remediation hint on the next line
Summary line always printed: 'N errors · N warnings · N checks passed'
Exit code 0 when all checks pass, 1 when warnings only, 2 when any error
doctor_impl accepts a generic io::Write writer so output can be captured in tests
Unit tests cover: all-pass output shape, single error output shape, single warning output shape, summary line counts
cargo test passes; cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean; cargo fmt --check clean; cargo deny check clean
userStories.notes: Reuse StatusFormatter<W> from spec 002 for ok/warn/err/kv primitives where possible. The section header and remediation-hint indentation are new output patterns — add them to StatusFormatter or implement a parallel DoctorFormatter if StatusFormatter's API makes it awkward.

userStories.title: --porcelain output, integration tests, and exit-code enforcement
userStories.description: Complete the `chronicle doctor` command with stable machine-readable output and end-to-end tests. Implement --porcelain mode: emit one `check.<key>=<state>[:<detail>]` line per check, then `summary.errors=N`, `summary.warnings=N`, `summary.passed=N`. Values follow `ok[:<detail>]`, `warning:<detail>`, `error:<detail>`. Keys are stable (no colour, no symbols). Write integration tests covering: (1) happy path — all checks pass, exit 0, output contains expected porcelain keys; (2) config error path — missing config file, exit 2, check.config.file=error; (3) agent warning path — sessions dir absent, exit 1 or 2, check.agents.<name>=error. Verify that --porcelain and human-readable modes both compile and run correctly end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: --porcelain emits one check.<key>=<state>[:<detail>] line per check in deterministic order
--porcelain emits summary.errors, summary.warnings, summary.passed as final three lines
Porcelain values follow ok[:<detail>], warning:<detail>, error:<detail> pattern with no ANSI codes
Skipped checks emit check.<key>=skipped[:<reason>]
Integration test: happy path runs chronicle doctor against a fully configured temp environment, exits 0, all porcelain lines are ok
Integration test: missing config file causes exit 2 and check.config.file=error:<detail> in porcelain output
Integration test: sessions dir absent for one agent causes check.agents.<name>=error:<detail> in output
All 3 integration tests pass in CI (no network required — remote reachability can be stubbed or the repo path set to a non-existent remote)
cargo test passes; cargo clippy -- -D warnings clean; cargo fmt --check clean; cargo deny check clean
userStories.notes: Integration tests live in tests/integration.rs alongside existing tests. For the remote-reachability check in integration tests, use a non-existent remote URL so the check fails fast (within the 5-second timeout) rather than requiring a real remote.
